name: @rello-platform/compliance-words
description: Canonical M7 prohibited-language source-of-truth: structured compliance vocabulary (token + category + context-rules) + a context-aware checker (negation/compound/disclaimer-banner aware, NOT a substring scan) + a committed cross-language keyset (dist/compliance-words-keyset.json) so TS and Python consumers enforce the same affirmative-vs-NOT-THAT logic at every publish/send/generate boundary.
comment:exports: Dual ESM+CJS build (tsup). This package has ZERO runtime dependencies (no transitive ESM-only @rello-platform import to break under require()), so a real CJS build is SAFE here — unlike @rello-platform/signals which must point require at its ESM file. A Milo CJS consumer (P5) require()s dist/index.cjs without ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED; ESM consumers get dist/index.js. The .json keyset subpath is exported so the Python Report-Engine (P3) can vendor it.

scripts.test: npm run build && node --test tests/*.test.mjs && npm run test:parity
